_metadata.title: BCI Regulatory Compliance Requirements
_metadata.description: Compliance requirements for BCI systems mapped across GDPR, CCPA, Chile Neurorights, UNESCO Recommendation, and Mind Act. Each requirement includes detection patterns, evidence criteria, and remediation guidance.
_metadata.status: Research reference. Requirements are derived from published regulatory text. This is not legal advice. Consult qualified legal counsel for compliance determinations.

compliance_domains.description: Neural data must be properly classified under applicable regulatory frameworks

requirements.requirement: Neural data classified as special category / sensitive data
requirements.description: EEG, ECoG, LFP, and other neural recordings linked to identifiable persons must be classified as biometric data (GDPR Art.9), sensitive personal information (CCPA), or organ tissue data (Chile Neurorights)
requirements.check: Verify data classification policy exists and covers neural data types
evidence: Data inventory including neural data types

requirements.requirement: Cognitive inference outputs classified as derived sensitive data
requirements.description: Outputs from cognitive state classification (emotion detection, attention scoring, mental state inference) must be classified at the same protection level as the source neural data
requirements.check: Verify derived data classification covers cognitive inference outputs

compliance_domains.description: Valid, informed consent must be obtained before neural data collection and processing

requirements.requirement: Explicit informed consent for neural data collection
requirements.description: Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous. For neural data, consent must explain: what data is collected, how it will be processed, who has access, retention period, and right to withdraw
requirements.check: Verify consent mechanism exists and covers all required elements

requirements.requirement: Separate consent for cognitive state inference
requirements.description: If the system infers cognitive states (emotions, attention, fatigue, intent) from neural data, separate consent is required for this processing beyond raw data collection
requirements.check: Verify separate consent for cognitive inference processing exists

requirements.requirement: Right to withdraw consent and data deletion
requirements.description: Users must be able to withdraw consent and request deletion of their neural data at any time, with deletion completed within regulatory timeframes
requirements.check: Verify withdrawal mechanism and deletion pipeline exist

compliance_domains.description: Neural data collection and processing must be limited to what is necessary for the stated purpose

requirements.requirement: Neural data collection limited to stated purpose
requirements.description: Only collect neural signals necessary for the declared purpose. Do not collect broader frequency bands, longer durations, or more channels than required
requirements.check: Verify data collection scope matches stated purpose

requirements.requirement: No secondary use without additional consent
requirements.description: Neural data collected for one purpose (e.g., neurofeedback therapy) must not be reused for another (e.g., marketing, research, training AI models) without obtaining new consent
requirements.check: Verify purpose limitation controls and secondary use policy

compliance_domains.description: Appropriate technical measures to protect neural data confidentiality, integrity, and availability

requirements.requirement: Encryption of neural data in transit
requirements.description: All neural data transmissions must be encrypted. Unencrypted protocols (raw LSL, unencrypted BLE, HTTP) are non-compliant for any identifiable neural data
requirements.check: Scan for unencrypted neural data transport patterns

requirements.requirement: Encryption of neural data at rest
requirements.description: Stored neural data files must be encrypted. Unencrypted EDF/BDF/XDF files containing identifiable data are non-compliant
requirements.check: Verify storage encryption for neural data files

requirements.description: Neural data access must be restricted to authorized personnel with documented justification. Principle of least privilege applies
requirements.check: Verify access control mechanisms for neural data stores

requirements.description: Devices that deliver stimulation must implement hardware and software safety limits to protect physical and mental integrity
requirements.check: Verify stimulation parameter limits and emergency shutoff

compliance_domains.description: Neural data must be anonymized when identification is not necessary for the processing purpose

requirements.requirement: Anonymization of neural data for research/analytics
requirements.description: When neural data is used for research, training, or analytics where individual identification is not needed, data must be anonymized to prevent re-identification
requirements.check: Verify anonymization procedures for non-clinical neural data use

requirements.description: If the system creates or stores neural biometric templates (brainwave signatures, EEG fingerprints), these require the highest protection level including encryption, access controls, and defined retention
requirements.check: Verify neural biometric template handling procedures

compliance_domains.description: Protection of cognitive autonomy and prevention of unauthorized mental state inference

requirements.description: The system must not coerce, manipulate, or unduly influence cognitive processes. Neurofeedback and stimulation must preserve user autonomy and agency
requirements.check: Verify cognitive autonomy protections in system design

requirements.description: The system must not infer mental states, preferences, or cognitive patterns beyond what the user has explicitly consented to. Incidental inference must be discarded, not stored or used
requirements.check: Verify mental privacy controls on inference pipelines

compliance_domains.description: Neural data transfers to third parties or across jurisdictions must meet regulatory requirements

requirements.requirement: Cross-border transfer safeguards for neural data
requirements.description: Transfers of neural data to jurisdictions without adequate protection require appropriate safeguards (standard contractual clauses, binding corporate rules, or adequacy decisions)
requirements.check: Verify transfer mechanisms for neural data crossing jurisdictional boundaries

requirements.description: Any sharing of neural data with third parties (cloud providers, analytics services, research partners) requires documented agreements covering data protection obligations
requirements.check: Verify data processing agreements with all third parties receiving neural data

compliance_domains.description: Procedures for responding to neural data breaches and security incidents

requirements.description: Breaches involving neural data must be reported to supervisory authorities within regulatory timeframes (72 hours under GDPR) and to affected individuals when high risk exists
requirements.check: Verify breach notification procedures cover neural data incidents
evidence: Incident response plan covering neural data

compliance_domains.description: Automated decisions based on neural data must be transparent and explainable

requirements.requirement: Transparency of neural data processing algorithms
requirements.description: Users must be informed about how their neural data is processed, what algorithms are used, and what decisions or outputs are derived from their data
requirements.check: Verify algorithmic transparency documentation for neural data processing
evidence: Model card or similar transparency artifact

critical.label: Critical
critical.description: Non-compliance creates immediate legal liability or physical safety risk. Must be addressed before deployment.

high.label: High
high.description: Non-compliance creates significant regulatory risk or privacy harm. Should be addressed within current development cycle.

medium.label: Medium
medium.description: Non-compliance creates moderate risk. Should be addressed within next planning period.

low.label: Low
low.description: Best practice recommendation. Non-compliance creates minimal immediate risk but may increase exposure over time.
